title: AtlaSent trust-root index
description: The signed index document at /.well-known/atlasent-trust-root.json. Lists the other trust-root resources, their hashes, the OIDC identity that signs them, and the index's own validity window. ADR-005 D3 makes valid_until required so verifiers can fail closed when the snapshot expires.

schema.description: Schema identifier and version. Hard-coded for forward-compatibility.

issued_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which this index was published.

valid_until.description: RFC 3339 timestamp after which verifiers MUST treat this index as expired. Per ADR-005 D3, default behaviour is fail-closed; callers may opt out with allow_expired_snapshot.

trust_root_signer.description: Identity that signed this index and the listed resources. Verifiers pin this string and reject signatures that don't match.

sigstore_identity.description: Fully-qualified OIDC subject of the publishing workflow.

oidc_issuer.description: OIDC issuer URL that minted the Sigstore identity token (typically https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com).

resources.description: The other trust-root files this index covers. Each entry carries its sha256 and the path to its detached cosign signature.

path.description: Absolute path on the trust-root host (e.g. /.well-known/atlasent-verifier-keys.json).

sha256.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 digest of the resource bytes.

sig.description: Absolute path to the detached cosign signature for the resource.

v2_anchors.description: Forward-looking anchors for V2 transparency-log integration. Verifiers MUST tolerate null and fall back to direct signature checks. Populated when V2 ships.

transparency_log_url.description: V2 transparency log URL. Null in V1.

transparency_log_pubkey.description: V2 transparency log public key, PEM-encoded. Null in V1.
